description: Links files stored in object storage (S3 or local filesystem) to any entity in any module. A purchase order can have attached quotes, an invoice can have a PDF, a supplier can have a contract scan. The Attachment record is the metadata — the file itself lives in object storage. Modules never manage file metadata directly.
routine: Attachment.attach(ctx, input) / Attachment.detach(ctx, id) / Attachment.findByEntity(ctx, input)
consumers: any module that needs to associate files with entities

EntityId.constraints: id of the entity this file is attached to

FileName.constraints: original file name as provided by the uploader. Max 255 chars. Stored for display — not used as storage key

MimeType.constraints: MIME type, e.g. 'application/pdf', 'image/png'. Validated against allowed types list in skill-hdm of the consuming module

SizeBytes.constraints: file size in bytes at upload time

StorageKey.constraints: object storage path, e.g. 'attachments/purchasing/PurchaseOrder/{entityId}/{id}.pdf'. Never exposed directly to frontend — access goes through a signed URL routine

StorageProvider.constraints: 'local' used in development and single-server installations. 's3' in production

Category.constraints: optional classification defined by the consuming module, e.g. 'quote', 'contract', 'proof-of-delivery'. Max 50 chars

UploadedBy.constraints: userId or agentId that uploaded the file

UploadedAt.constraints: set by MDM routine at write time

DeletedAt.constraints: soft delete — file is not removed from storage immediately. A cleanup job removes the object after the grace period

Details.constraints: optional metadata from the consuming module — e.g. page count, document version, NF-e access key for XML attachments. Stored in DynamoDB only. Max 50KB

postgresql.note: list all attachments for an entity
list attachments by category, e.g. all quotes for a PO
cleanup job — find soft-deleted files past grace period

dynamodb.note: full attachment record including Details — used for restore and audit trail

consistencyContract.note: index fields sync — file must be findable immediately after upload

retention.postgresql: soft delete via DeletedAt — hard delete from PostgreSQL after 30 days grace period
retention.dynamodb: permanent — attachment history preserved even after file deletion
retention.storage: object deleted from S3/local after grace period by cleanup job

persistence.note: only DeletedAt is writable after creation — FileName, StorageKey and other fields never change
